hook.text: Chat apps update instantly. Stock tickers stream live. HTTP's request-response pattern can't do that alone.

buildup.text: WebSocket starts as an HTTP request, then upgrades to a full-duplex, persistent connection.

discovery.text: Once open, either side can send messages at any time. No polling, no waiting, just real-time data flow.

twist.text: WebSocket connections stay open for minutes or hours. That's great for users but costly for servers at scale.

climax.text: Slack, Discord, live sports — any app with instant updates likely uses WebSockets or something similar.

punchline.text: WebSocket keeps the line open so data flows freely.

quiz.question: How does WebSocket differ from standard HTTP?
options: It maintains a persistent two-way connection
It uses a completely different port than HTTP
It encrypts data without needing TLS
